input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS, targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **Command Line Tools** after:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  ```
- Open Xcode once to accept the license and install additional components.
- **Discover version:** MAUI docs list the minimum Xcode version per .NET release. Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui) → supported platforms table.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release (e.g., .NET 9).
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel.
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet --version
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and all transitive dependencies (Android SDK, build tools, etc.).
- **Discover available workloads:**
  ```
  dotnet workload search maui
  ```
- **Verify installed workloads:**
  ```
  dotnet workload list
  ```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload auto-installs the Android SDK via the `maui-android` workload. To confirm or customize:

- **Default location:** `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` or `~/Android/Sdk`
- Set `ANDROID_HOME` if needed:
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  ```
- To install additional API levels or emulator images, use Android **SDK Manager** (bundled with Android Studio, or the `sdkmanager` CLI in the SDK's `cmdline-tools`).
- **Optional:** Install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) for emulator management and a visual SDK manager.

## 5. iOS Simulator Setup

- Comes with Xcode. Open **Xcode → Settings → Platforms** to download additional simulator runtimes (e.g., iOS 17, 18).
- **Verify simulators:**
  ```
  xcrun simctl list devices available
  ```

## 6. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (has MAUI support) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the MAUI extension is now the official Microsoft-recommended editor on macOS.

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build check
dotnet build

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Full Verification Checklist

```bash
dotnet --version                    # SDK installed
dotnet workload list                # "maui" present
xcodebuild -version                # Xcode version + build
xcrun simctl list devices available # iOS simulators
adb devices                        # Android emulator/device visible (if running)
dotnet new maui -n VerifySetup && cd VerifySetup && dotnet build  # builds clean
```

## Version Discovery Strategy

- **Compatibility matrix:** Always check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms) — it maps .NET version → required Xcode version → supported iOS/Android API levels.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.

---

**Install order matters:** Xcode first → .NET SDK → MAUI workload → verify. This avoids issues where the MAUI workload can't find Apple toolchains during installation.
